title: artemisia ludoviciana subsp. albula
intro: Artemisia ludoviciana subsp. albula, also known as White Sagebrush, is a perennial shrub native to North America, characterized by its silvery-gray foliage and small yellow flowers. This plant has been used in herbal medicine for its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antiviral properties, which can help alleviate symptoms of colds, fever, and respiratory issues. The health benefits of White Sagebrush include its ability to boost the immune system, reduce inflammation, and promote relaxation, making it a popular ingredient in herbal teas and remedies. White Sagebrush can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or salve, and is often used in conjunction with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties.
